mnemonics.title: Order of Operations
mnemonics.content: Please Excuse My Dear Aunt Sally (Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division, and Addition and Subtraction)

mnemonics.title: Comparison Operators
mnemonics.content: Less than (<), Greater than (>), Less than or Equal to (<=), Greater than or Equal to (>=), Equal to (==), Not Equal to (!=)

acronyms.expansion: Parentheses, Exponents, Multiplication and Division, and Addition and Subtraction

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a number line with the numbers increasing from left to right. The comparison operators can be visualized as follows: Less than (<) means to the left, Greater than (>) means to the right, Less than or Equal to (<=) means to the left or at the same point, Greater than or Equal to (>=) means to the right or at the same point.

Imagine a light switch with two positions: on (True) and off (False). The logical operators can be visualized as follows: And (and) means both switches must be on, Or (or) means at least one switch must be on, Not (not) means the opposite of the switch position.

analogies.analogy: Imagine a club with members. The membership operators can be visualized as follows: In (in) means the person is a member of the club, Not In (not in) means the person is not a member of the club.
Imagine two identical twins. The identity operators can be visualized as follows: Is (is) means the twins are the same person, Is Not (is not) means the twins are not the same person.
